text: The old harbor town of Essaouira sits on Morocco's Atlantic coast where the wind never quite stops moving. Whitewashed walls rise close together along narrow lanes, and the smell of salt and cedar mingles with charcoal smoke from the fish grills along the port. Blue wooden boats rock against the seawall, and in the late afternoon the light turns everything the color of faded saffron, soft and a little melancholy, as though the town has been slowly dreaming for centuries and has no particular intention of waking up.
